name: greyhorse
documentation:
Greyhorse core library
======================

Modular framework for building pythonic microservices


How to build
------------


- Install the project

    ``uv python pin 3.12.1``

    ``uv venv``

    ``uv sync``

    ``source .venv/bin/activate``



- Format code commands

    ``ruff check --unsafe-fixes --fix``

    ``ruff format``

functions.name: greyhorse.utils.time.calc_next_interval
documentation:
The function counts the next time interval in seconds with progressive increase

initial_period: Initial value of the period.
iteration: Current iteration number.
iteration_divisor: Iteration divider, for the ability to change the interval
not every iteration, but after several.
max_seconds: Maximum period duration in seconds.

modules.name: greyhorse.maybe
documentation: mypy: warn_no_return=false,disable_error_code="arg-type,has-type,misc,return-value"

functions.name: greyhorse.maybe.is_just
documentation:
A typeguard to check if a Maybe is a Just

Usage:
\>>> r: Maybe[int] = get_a_maybe()
\>>> if is_just(r):
\>>> r # r is of type Just[int]
\>>> elif is_nothing(r):
\>>> r # r is of type Nothing

functions.name: greyhorse.maybe.is_nothing
documentation:
A typeguard to check if a Maybe is a Nothing

Usage:
\>>> r: Maybe[int] = get_a_maybe()
\>>> if is_just(r):
\>>> r # r is of type Just[int]
\>>> elif is_nothing(r):
\>>> r # r is of type Nothing

methods.name: greyhorse.maybe.Maybe.is_just
documentation: Returns true if the `Maybe` is a `Just` value.

methods.name: greyhorse.maybe.Maybe.is_just_and
documentation: Returns true if the `Maybe` is a `Just` and the value inside of it matches a predicate.

methods.name: greyhorse.maybe.Maybe.is_nothing
documentation: Returns true if the `Maybe` is a `Nothing` value.

methods.name: greyhorse.maybe.Maybe.ok_or
documentation:
Transforms the `Maybe[T]` into a `Result[T, E]`, mapping `Just(v)` to `Ok(v)`
and `Nothing` to `Err(err)`.

methods.name: greyhorse.maybe.Maybe.ok_or_else
documentation:
Transforms the `Maybe[T]` into a `Result[T, E]`, mapping `Just(v)` to `Ok(v)`
and `Nothing` to `Err(err())`.

methods.name: greyhorse.maybe.Maybe.inspect
documentation:
Calls a function with the contained value if `Just`.
Returns the original `Maybe`.

methods.name: greyhorse.maybe.Maybe.expect
documentation:
Returns the contained `Just` value if the `Maybe` is a `Just` or raises a
`MaybeUnwrapError`.

methods.name: greyhorse.maybe.Maybe.unwrap
documentation:
Returns the contained value if the `Maybe` is a `Just` or raises a
`MaybeUnwrapError`.

methods.name: greyhorse.maybe.Maybe.unwrap_or
documentation: Returns the contained `Just` value or a provided default.

methods.name: greyhorse.maybe.Maybe.unwrap_or_none
documentation: Returns the contained `Just` value or `None`.

methods.name: greyhorse.maybe.Maybe.unwrap_or_else
documentation: Returns the contained `Just` value or computes it from a closure.

methods.name: greyhorse.maybe.Maybe.unwrap_or_raise
documentation: Returns the contained `Just` value or raise the provided exception.

methods.name: greyhorse.maybe.Maybe.map
documentation:
Maps a `Maybe[T]` to `Maybe[U]` by applying a function to a contained value (if `Just`)
or returns `Nothing` (if `Nothing`).

methods.name: greyhorse.maybe.Maybe.map_async
documentation:
Maps a `Maybe[T]` to `Maybe[U]` by applying a function to a contained value (if `Just`)
or returns `Nothing` (if `Nothing`).

methods.name: greyhorse.maybe.Maybe.map_or
documentation:
Returns the provided default result (if `Nothing`), or applies a function
to the contained value (if `Just`).

methods.name: greyhorse.maybe.Maybe.map_or_else
documentation:
Computes a default function result (if `Nothing`), or applies a different function
to the contained value (if `Just`).

methods.name: greyhorse.maybe.Maybe.and_
documentation: Returns `Nothing` if the Maybe is `Nothing`, otherwise returns `other`.

methods.name: greyhorse.maybe.Maybe.and_then
documentation:
Returns `Nothing` if the Maybe is `Nothing`, otherwise calls `f` with the
wrapped value and returns the result. Some languages call this operation flatmap.

methods.name: greyhorse.maybe.Maybe.and_then_async
documentation:
Returns `Nothing` if the Maybe is `Nothing`, otherwise calls `f` with the
wrapped value and returns the result. Some languages call this operation flatmap.

methods.name: greyhorse.maybe.Maybe.filter
documentation:
Returns `Nothing` if the Maybe is `Nothing`, otherwise calls `predicate` with the
wrapped value and returns:
* `Just(t)` if predicate returns true (where t is the wrapped value), and
* `Nothing` if predicate returns false.

methods.name: greyhorse.maybe.Maybe.or_
documentation: Returns the Maybe if it contains a value, otherwise returns `other`.

methods.name: greyhorse.maybe.Maybe.or_else
documentation: Returns the Maybe if it contains a value, otherwise calls `f` and returns the result.

methods.name: greyhorse.maybe.Maybe.xor
documentation: Returns `Just` if exactly one of `self`, `other` is `Just`, otherwise returns `Nothing`.

methods.name: greyhorse.maybe.Maybe.flatten
documentation: Converts from `Maybe[Maybe[T]]` to `Maybe[T]`.

methods.name: greyhorse.maybe.Maybe.to_result
documentation:
Transposes a `Maybe` of a `Result` into an `Result` of a `Maybe`.

`Nothing` will be mapped to `Ok(Nothing)`.
`Just(Ok(_))` and `Just(Err(_))` will be mapped to `Ok(Just(_))` and `Err(_)`.

classes.name: greyhorse.maybe.MaybeUnwrapError
documentation: Exception raised from `.unwrap_<...>` and `.expect_<...>` calls.

modules.name: greyhorse.result
documentation: mypy: warn_no_return=false,disable_error_code="arg-type,has-type,misc,return-value"

functions.name: greyhorse.result.is_ok
documentation:
A typeguard to check if a result is an Ok

Usage:
\>>> r: Result[int, str] = get_a_result()
\>>> if is_ok(r):
\>>> r # r is of type Ok[int]
\>>> elif is_err(r):
\>>> r # r is of type Err[str]

functions.name: greyhorse.result.is_err
documentation:
A typeguard to check if a result is an Err

Usage:
\>>> r: Result[int, str] = get_a_result()
\>>> if is_ok(r):
\>>> r # r is of type Ok[int]
\>>> elif is_err(r):
\>>> r # r is of type Err[str]

functions.name: greyhorse.result.as_result_sync
documentation:
Make a decorator to turn a function into one that returns a `Result`.

Regular return values are turned into `Ok(return_value)`. Raised
exceptions of the specified exception type(s) are turned into `Err(exc)`.

functions.name: greyhorse.result.as_result_async
documentation:
Make a decorator to turn an async function into one that returns a `Result`.
Regular return values are turned into `Ok(return_value)`. Raised
exceptions of the specified exception type(s) are turned into `Err(exc)`.

functions.name: greyhorse.result.do
greyhorse.result.do_async

type.name: Generator[Result[T, E], None, None] | AsyncGenerator[Result[T, E], None]

documentation:
Async version of do. Example:

``` python
final_result: Result[float, int] = await do_async(
    Ok(len(x) + int(y) + z)
    for x in await get_async_result_1()
    for y in await get_async_result_2()
    for z in get_sync_result_3()
)
```

NOTE: Python makes generators async in a counter-intuitive way.

``` python
# This is a regular generator:
async def foo(): ...
    do(Ok(1) for x in await foo())
```

``` python
# But this is an async generator:
async def foo(): ...
async def bar(): ...


do(Ok(1) for x in await foo() for y in await bar())
```

We let users try to use regular `do()`, which works in some cases
of awaiting async values. If we hit a case like above, we raise
an exception telling the user to use `do_async()` instead.
See `do()`.

However, for better usability, it's better for `do_async()` to also accept
regular generators, as you get in the first case:

``` python
async def foo(): ...
    do(Ok(1) for x in await foo())
```

Furthermore, neither mypy nor pyright can infer that the second case is
actually an async generator, so we cannot annotate `do_async()`
as accepting only an async generator. This is additional motivation
to accept either.

documentation: Returns true if the `Result` is `Ok`.

methods.name: greyhorse.result.Result.ok
documentation: Converts the `Result` into a `Maybe[T]` and discarding the error, if any.

methods.name: greyhorse.result.Result.err
documentation: Converts the `Result` into a `Maybe[E]` and discarding the success value, if any.

methods.name: greyhorse.result.Result.inspect
documentation:
Calls a function with the contained value if `Ok`.
Returns the original `Result`.

methods.name: greyhorse.result.Result.inspect_err
documentation:
Calls a function with the contained value if `Err`.
Returns the original `Result`.

methods.name: greyhorse.result.Result.expect
documentation:
Returns the contained `Ok` value if the `Result` is `Ok` or raises a
`ResultUnwrapError`.

methods.name: greyhorse.result.Result.expect_err
documentation:
Returns the contained `Err` value if the `Result` is `Err` or raises a
`ResultUnwrapError`.

methods.name: greyhorse.result.Result.unwrap
documentation:
Returns the contained `Ok` value if the `Result` is `Ok` or raises a
`ResultUnwrapError`.

methods.name: greyhorse.result.Result.unwrap_err
documentation:
Returns the contained `Err` value if the `Result` is `Err` or raises a
`ResultUnwrapError`.

methods.name: greyhorse.result.Result.unwrap_or
documentation: Returns the contained `Ok` value or a provided default.

methods.name: greyhorse.result.Result.unwrap_or_none
documentation: Returns the contained `Ok` value or `None`.

methods.name: greyhorse.result.Result.unwrap_err_or_none
documentation: Returns the contained `Err` value or `None`.

methods.name: greyhorse.result.Result.unwrap_or_else
documentation: Returns the contained `Ok` value or computes it from a closure.

methods.name: greyhorse.result.Result.unwrap_or_raise
documentation: Returns the contained `Ok` value or raise the provided exception.

methods.name: greyhorse.result.Result.map
documentation:
Maps a `Result[T, E]` to `Result[U, E]` by applying a function to a
contained `Ok` value, leaving an `Err` value untouched.

This function can be used to compose the results of two functions.

methods.name: greyhorse.result.Result.map_async
documentation:
Maps a `Result[T, E]` to `Result[U, E]` by applying a function to a
contained `Ok` value, leaving an `Err` value untouched.

This function can be used to compose the results of two functions.

methods.name: greyhorse.result.Result.map_or
documentation:
Returns the provided default (if `Err`), or applies a function
to the contained value (if `Ok`).

methods.name: greyhorse.result.Result.map_or_else
documentation:
Maps a `Result[T, E]` to `U` by applying fallback function `default` to a
contained `Err` value, or function `f` to a contained `Ok` value.

This function can be used to unpack a successful result while handling an error.

methods.name: greyhorse.result.Result.map_err
documentation:
Maps a `Result[T, E]` to `Result[T, F]` by applying a function to a
contained `Err` value, leaving an `Ok` value untouched.

This function can be used to pass through a successful result while handling an error.

methods.name: greyhorse.result.Result.and_
documentation: Returns `other` if the Result is `Ok`, otherwise returns the `Err` value of `self`.

methods.name: greyhorse.result.Result.and_then
documentation:
Calls `f` if the result is `Ok`, otherwise returns the `Err` value of `self`.
This function can be used for control flow based on `Result` values.

methods.name: greyhorse.result.Result.and_then_async
documentation:
Calls `f` if the result is `Ok`, otherwise returns the `Err` value of `self`.
This function can be used for control flow based on `Result` values.

methods.name: greyhorse.result.Result.or_
documentation: Returns `other` if the Result is `Err`, otherwise returns the `Ok` value of `self`.

methods.name: greyhorse.result.Result.or_else
documentation:
Calls `f` if the result is `Err`, otherwise returns the `Ok` value of `self`.
This function can be used for control flow based on `Result` values.

methods.name: greyhorse.result.Result.flatten
documentation: Converts from `Result[Result[T, E], E]` to `Result[T, E]`.

methods.name: greyhorse.result.Result.to_maybe
documentation:
Transposes a `Result` of an `Maybe` into an `Maybe` of a `Result`.

`Ok(Nothing)` will be mapped to `Nothing`.
`Ok(Just(_))` and `Err(_)` will be mapped to `Just(Ok(_))` and `Just(Err(_))`.

classes.name: greyhorse.result.ResultUnwrapError

methods.name: greyhorse.result.ResultUnwrapError.value
documentation: Returns the contained value.

Exception raised from `.unwrap_<...>` and `.expect_<...>` calls.

The contained value can be accessed via the `.value` attribute, but
this is not intended for regular use, as type information is lost:
`ResultUnwrapError` doesn't know about both `T` and `E`, since it's raised
from `Ok()` or `Err()` which only knows about either `T` or `E`,
not both.

classes.name: greyhorse.result.DoError
documentation:
This is used to signal to `do()` that the result is an `Err`,
which short-circuits the generator and returns that Err.
Using this exception for control flow in `do()` allows us
to simulate `and_then()` in the Err case: namely, we don't call `op`,
we just return `self` (the Err).
